dsl_rule.translation_notes: Channel breakout operator translated using Donchian Channel (dc_upper and dc_lower) which represents the n-period highest high and lowest low over a lookback period. The dc_upper approximates the channel top and dc_lower approximates the channel bottom. Entry conditions trigger on price closing above/below these extremes. Exit conditions reverse when price closes back inside the channel. The 10-period lookback and 1-bar confirmation are implicit in the Donchian calculation. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to reflect that the signal generates both long (above breakout) and short (below breakout) signals based on market conditions.

backtest_rule.reason: Channel breakout operator translated using Donchian Channel (dc_upper and dc_lower) which represents the n-period highest high and lowest low over a lookback period. The dc_upper approximates the channel top and dc_lower approximates the channel bottom. Entry conditions trigger on price closing above/below these extremes. Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to reflect that the signal generates both long (above breakout) and short (below breakout) signals based on market conditions.

source_rule_text: A trader should use channel-breakout operators to filter noise and identify the current trend direction in a time series before entering trend-following trades.
